formulas.name: Calculation of Gross Profit from Incomplete Records
formulas.notes: Use this formula when gross profit percentage is known
formulas.formula: Gross Profit = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit Percentage of Cost of Goods Sold

variables.Gross Profit: Profit earned on sale of goods
variables.Gross Profit Percentage: Percentage of gross profit on cost of goods sold

formulas.name: Calculation of Gross Profit Percentage from Incomplete Records
formulas.notes: Use this formula when gross profit is known
formulas.formula: Gross Profit Percentage = (Gross Profit / Cost of Goods Sold) × 100

variables.Gross Profit: Profit earned on sale of goods
variables.Gross Profit Percentage: Percentage of gross profit on cost of goods sold

formulas.name: Calculation of Cost of Goods Sold from Incomplete Records
formulas.notes: Use this formula to calculate cost of goods sold when opening and closing stock values are known
formulas.formula: Cost of Goods Sold = (Opening Stock + Purchase + Direct Expenses) - Closing Stock

formulas.name: Calculation of Net Profit from Incomplete Records
formulas.notes: Use this formula when gross profit, indirect expenses, and gross profit from other businesses are known
formulas.formula: Net Profit = Gross Profit - Indirect Expenses + Gross Profit from Other Businesses

variables.Net Profit: Profit earned by business after deducting expenses
variables.Gross Profit: Profit earned on sale of goods

formulas.name: Statement of Affairs
formulas.notes: Use this statement to determine the financial position of a business
formulas.formula: Statement of Affairs is a statement showing the financial position of a business at a given date.

formulas.name: Reconstitution of Accounts from Incomplete Records
formulas.notes: Use this method to prepare accounts when records are incomplete
formulas.formula: Reconstitution of accounts involves the preparation of accounts from incomplete records using various methods.
